sentences.base_language: I wake up at seven in the morning.
sentences.target_language: Me despierto a las siete de la mañana.
sentences.grammarNote: **Despertarse** is a reflexive verb: **me despierto** (I wake up), **te despiertas**, **se despierta**. Many daily routine verbs are reflexive: **levantarse** (to get up), **ducharse** (to shower), **vestirse** (to get dressed), **acostarse** (to go to bed).

sentences.base_language: I get up and go to the bathroom.
sentences.target_language: Me levanto y voy al baño.
sentences.grammarNote:
**Me levanto** — another reflexive daily routine verb: **levantarse** (to get up/get out of bed).
**al baño** = **a + el baño** — the contraction **al** is mandatory whenever **a** precedes **el**.

sentences.base_language: I wash my face and brush my teeth.
sentences.target_language: Me lavo la cara y me cepillo los dientes.
sentences.grammarNote: With body-care verbs, Spanish uses reflexive + definite article instead of a possessive: **me lavo la cara** (I wash my face), not *lavo mi cara*. The reflexive pronoun (**me/te/se**) already indicates whose body part it is.

**Desayunar**, **almorzar**, and **cenar** can be used as verbs in Spanish (not just nouns): **desayuno** (I have breakfast), **almuerzo** (I have lunch), **ceno** (I have dinner). No extra verb needed.
**Luego** = then/next in a sequence — also means 'later' in **hasta luego**.

sentences.base_language: I drink a cup of coffee.
sentences.grammarNote:
**Tomar** = to take/drink — broader than **beber** (to drink) in Latin America. **Tomo café** = I drink coffee.
**una taza de café** — note **de** (of/a cup of): quantities and containers use **de**: **un vaso de agua**, **una botella de vino**.

**Vestirse** is a reflexive e→i stem-changing verb: **me visto** · **te vistes** · **se viste**.
**rápido** here is used as an adverb (quickly) — informally, the adjective form is used instead of the adverb **rápidamente**. This is common in speech.

sentences.base_language: I say goodbye and leave the house.
sentences.target_language: Me despido y salgo de casa.
sentences.grammarNote:
**salgo** is the irregular yo form of **salir** (to leave/go out). **Salgo de casa** = I leave home. Other irregular yo forms: **hago** (hacer), **tengo** (tener), **vengo** (venir), **pongo** (poner), **digo** (decir).
**Me despido** = I say goodbye — from **despedirse** (reflexive), literally 'to take one's leave'.

sentences.base_language: I walk to work every day.
sentences.target_language: Camino al trabajo todos los días.
sentences.grammarNote:
**Caminar** = to walk (regular -ar verb).
**todos los días** = every day (literally 'all the days') — the most natural way to say 'every day' in Spanish.
**al trabajo** = **a + el trabajo** — mandatory contraction.
